concept_name: What Is a Magnetic Field? — B as a Vector Field Sourced by Moving Charge
source_book: DC Pandey Class 12 Ch.4 Moving Charges and Magnetism — table-of-contents reference only (the chapter-opening 'what is a magnetic field' idea that precedes §4.4 magnitude); teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor only. This concept slots BEFORE magnetic_field_wire (§4.4): it establishes WHAT a magnetic field IS — a genuine vector field that exists at every point of space, sourced by MOVING charge (current), revealed (not created) by a compass — exactly as the electric field is a vector field sourced by a still charge and revealed by a test charge. It deliberately does NOT teach the magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr); that numeric lesson belongs to magnetic_field_wire. The atomic claim: a moving charge fills the space around it with a vector field B that CIRCULATES around the wire (azimuthal, tangent, perpendicular to the wire), just as a static charge fills space with the radial vector field E. Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal). Renders on field_3d via the straight_wire_current scenario; the STATE_2/3 compass on→off→on toggle and the STATE_6 side-by-side E-vs-B comparison panel are FLAGGED to renderer_primitives as new scenario support.

real_world_anchor.primary: Lay a cheap magnetic compass beside the wire of your phone charger while the phone is charging. The needle twitches a little off north — and snaps straight back to north the instant you unplug it. There is no magnet anywhere near it. The moving charge in the wire — the current — reached out into the space around the wire and pushed the needle. Switch the current on and off, and the field appears and vanishes with it.
real_world_anchor.secondary: In 1820 Hans Christian Oersted noticed his compass needle twitch every time he ran a current through a nearby wire. That tiny twitch was the birth of electromagnetism — proof that a moving charge makes a magnetic field. You can repeat his experiment on a desk with a battery, a wire, and a compass.

r.name: Distance from the wire to the field-probe point

Q.name: Source charge (STATE_6 analogy panel — display only)

q.name: Positive test charge that reveals E (STATE_6 analogy — display only)

formulas.field_is_vector: B is a genuine vector field: at every point of space it has a size and a direction
formulas.source_is_moving_charge: The source of B is moving charge — a current. A charge at rest makes no magnetic field.
formulas.direction_circulates: B circulates AROUND the wire — it is tangent to the circle through the point, perpendicular to the wire, never along it
formulas.strength_relation_qualitative: B is stronger nearer the wire and stronger with more current (the magnitude itself is taught in the next concept)
formulas.compass_reveals: A compass is a test object: it REVEALS the field that is already there — it does not create it
formulas.E_B_analogy: A still charge fills space with a radial field E; a moving charge fills space with a circulating field B — both are vector fields revealed by a test object

constraints: Steady DC current is assumed throughout
Long straight wire idealization: B is azimuthal only — it circulates around the wire with zero radial and zero axial component
The source of B is moving charge (current); a charge at rest produces zero magnetic field
A compass is an ideal test dipole that REVEALS the field, it does not create it
B is a genuine vector field — defined with a size and direction at every point of space, whether or not a compass is there
The magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) is grounding only here; the numeric magnitude lesson belongs to magnetic_field_wire and is never shown on-canvas in this concept
B_magnitude at I = 0 is cleanly zero (STATE_3 is reachable because I has min 0)

aha_moment.statement: A moving charge circles space with B, as a still charge fills space with E.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Both panels reveal a vector field with a test object — radial E on the left, circulating B on the right; same idea, two sources.

STATE_1.title: The Source — a current starts to flow

scene_composition.text: Wire + battery — switch open, no current yet

current dots flow UP
when the switch closes

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a plain wire joined to a battery. The switch is open, so no current flows yet — nothing is moving.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक simple wire है जो battery से जुड़ी है। switch अभी open है, इसलिए अभी कोई current नहीं बह रहा — कुछ भी move नहीं हो रहा।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ ఒక simple wire ఉంది, battery కి connect అయి ఉంది. switch ఇప్పుడు open గా ఉంది, కాబట్టి ఇంకా ఏ current ప్రవహించడం లేదు — ఏమీ move అవ్వడం లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now the switch closes. Watch the yellow dots — charge starts moving up the wire. That moving charge is the current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब switch close होता है। yellow dots को देखिए — charge wire के ऊपर move करना शुरू करता है। वही moving charge current है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు switch close అవుతుంది. yellow dots చూడండి — charge wire పై పైకి move అవ్వడం మొదలవుతుంది. ఆ moving charge నే current అంటాం.

tts_sentences.text_en: Hold on to this one idea: the source we are studying is moving charge. Not a magnet — a current.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक idea याद रखिए: हम जो source पढ़ रहे हैं वह moving charge है। magnet नहीं — current है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ ఒక్క idea గుర్తు పెట్టుకోండి: మనం చదువుతున్న source అంటే moving charge. magnet కాదు — current.

scene_composition.text: Predict: which way will the needle turn?
needle swings to the LOCAL field direction

tts_sentences.text_en: Bring a compass next to the live wire. At rest, its needle simply points north.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक compass को live wire के पास लाइए। rest में इसकी needle बस north की ओर इशारा करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక compass ని live wire దగ్గర తీసుకు రండి. rest లో దాని needle కేవలం north వైపు చూపిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Before I show you — predict. With current flowing, which way do you think the needle will swing? Take a moment.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दिखाने से पहले — predict करिए। current बहते हुए, आपको क्या लगता है needle किस तरफ swing करेगी? एक पल सोचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు చూపించే ముందు — predict చేయండి. current ప్రవహిస్తుండగా, needle ఏ వైపు swing అవుతుందని మీకు అనిపిస్తుంది? ఒక్క నిమిషం ఆలోచించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch. The needle swings away from north and lines up tangent to the field that circles the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए। needle north से दूर swing करती है और wire के चारों ओर circle करने वाले field के tangent में line up हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి. needle north నుండి దూరంగా swing అవుతుంది మరియు wire చుట్టూ circle చేస్తున్న field కి tangent గా line up అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is exactly what Oersted saw in 1820. A current — moving charge — bends the compass. That was the birth of electromagnetism.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही Oersted ने 1820 में देखा था। एक current — moving charge — compass को मोड़ देता है। वह electromagnetism का जन्म था।
tts_sentences.text_te: 1820 లో Oersted చూసింది సరిగ్గా ఇదే. ఒక current — moving charge — compass ని వంచింది. అదే electromagnetism పుట్టిన క్షణం.

misconception_watch.belief: The compass needle just points straight at the wire, like it is being pulled toward it
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The needle settles tangent to the circle around the wire, not radially toward it.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The needle aligns with the LOCAL field direction, which circles the wire — it does not point at the wire.

scene_composition.text: Switch opens — current goes to zero

Predict: what happens
to the needle now?
no current → no field. There is no magnet here.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now I open the switch. The current drops to zero — the moving charge stops.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब मैं switch खोलता हूँ। current zero हो जाता है — moving charge रुक जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు నేను switch తెరుస్తున్నాను. current zero కి పడిపోతుంది — moving charge ఆగిపోతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Before it happens — predict. With the current gone, what will the compass needle do? Think about it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: होने से पहले — predict करिए। current चले जाने पर compass needle क्या करेगी? इसके बारे में सोचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది జరిగే ముందు — predict చేయండి. current పోయాక compass needle ఏమి చేస్తుంది? ఆలోచించండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It snaps straight back to north. The field vanished the instant the current stopped.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह सीधे north पर वापस snap होती है। current रुकने के instant ही field गायब हो गई।
tts_sentences.text_te: అది నేరుగా north కి snap అవుతుంది. current ఆగిన instant లోనే field అదృశ్యమైంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the field never came from a magnet. It came from the moving charge. Stop the charge, and the field is gone.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो field कभी magnet से नहीं आई। वह moving charge से आई। charge को रोकिए, और field चली जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి field ఎప్పుడూ magnet నుండి రాలేదు. అది moving charge నుండి వచ్చింది. charge ని ఆపండి, field పోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: There must be a hidden magnet somewhere making the field
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The needle returns to north the moment the current stops — nothing magnetic remains in the scene.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: A magnetic field needs moving charge, not a magnet. No current means no field.

scene_composition.text: Predict: at a NEW spot, will the needle turn the same way?

move the compass
to another point
the field is at EVERY point — not just where the compass sits

tts_sentences.text_en: Switch the current back on and move the compass to a brand-new spot near the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: current को वापस on करें और compass को wire के पास एक बिल्कुल नई जगह ले जाएं।
tts_sentences.text_te: current ని మళ్ళీ on చేయండి మరియు compass ని wire దగ్గర ఒక కొత్త spot కి move చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first: at this new point, does the needle turn the same way, or a different way? Take a second.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करिए: इस नए point पर, needle उसी तरफ मुड़ती है, या अलग तरफ? एक पल लीजिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: ముందు predict చేయండి: ఈ కొత్త point వద్ద, needle అదే వైపు తిరుగుతుందా, లేదా వేరే వైపా? ఒక second తీసుకోండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: It deflects again — but tangent to the circle at THIS point, so it lines up in a different direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वह फिर deflect होती है — लेकिन इस point पर circle के tangent में, इसलिए एक अलग direction में line up हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అది మళ్ళీ deflect అవుతుంది — కానీ ఈ point వద్ద circle కి tangent గా, కాబట్టి ఒక వేరే direction లో line up అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The field was already there, at every point in the space. The compass only reveals what was waiting at each spot.
tts_sentences.text_hi: field पहले से ही वहाँ थी, space के हर point पर। compass केवल वह reveal करता है जो हर spot पर wait कर रहा था।
tts_sentences.text_te: field అప్పటికే అక్కడ ఉంది, space లోని ప్రతి point వద్ద. compass కేవలం ప్రతి spot వద్ద వేచి ఉన్నది reveal చేస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The field only exists at the one place where the compass is sitting
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The compass hops to many points and deflects at each — faint tangent field markers appear everywhere, not just under the needle.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: The field exists at every point of space; the compass just reveals it wherever you place it.

scene_composition.text: B circulates AROUND the wire — full 3D rings

B is tangent, perpendicular
to the wire — never along it

a vector at EVERY point
= a vector field

tts_sentences.text_en: Now show the whole field at once. Tangent arrows appear all around the wire and join into full 3D rings.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब पूरा field एक साथ दिखाते हैं। tangent arrows wire के चारों ओर appear होते हैं और full 3D rings में जुड़ जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మొత్తం field ఒకేసారి చూపిద్దాం. tangent arrows wire చుట్టూ appear అవుతాయి మరియు full 3D rings లో చేరతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every arrow circulates around the wire. None points along the wire, and none points straight out from it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर arrow wire के चारों ओर circulate करता है। कोई भी wire के साथ point नहीं करता, और कोई भी उससे सीधे बाहर point नहीं करता।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి arrow wire చుట్టూ circulate అవుతుంది. ఏ arrow కూడా wire వెంట point చేయదు, మరియు ఏదీ దాని నుండి నేరుగా బయటకు point చేయదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: B is perpendicular to the wire, tangent to the circle through each point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: B wire के perpendicular है, हर point से गुजरने वाले circle के tangent में।
tts_sentences.text_te: B wire కి perpendicular గా ఉంటుంది, ప్రతి point గుండా వెళ్ళే circle కి tangent గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: A size and a direction at every point of space — that is exactly what we mean by a vector field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: space के हर point पर एक size और एक direction — यही हमारा vector field से मतलब है।
tts_sentences.text_te: space లోని ప్రతి point వద్ద ఒక size మరియు ఒక direction — ఇదే మనం vector field అని అర్థం చేసుకుంటాం.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic field points along the wire, in the same direction as the current
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Rotate the view: every B arrow is tangent to a ring around the wire, never parallel to it.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: B circulates around the wire — perpendicular to it, tangent to the circle, never along the wire.

scene_composition.text:
still +Q: E points straight OUT
(test charge q reveals it)

moving charge: B CIRCLES around
(compass reveals it)
both are vector fields — a test object reveals each
still charge → radial E   ·   moving charge → circulating B

tts_sentences.text_en: Put the two side by side. On the left, a still charge Q with a test charge q feeling a force.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों को side by side रखते हैं। left पर एक still charge Q है, जिसे एक test charge q force feel कर रहा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండింటిని side by side పెడదాం. left వైపు ఒక still charge Q ఉంది, దానిని ఒక test charge q force feel చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The test charge reveals the electric field E, pointing straight out from the charge in every direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: test charge electric field E को reveal करता है, जो charge से हर direction में सीधे बाहर point करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: test charge electric field E ని reveal చేస్తుంది, ఇది charge నుండి ప్రతి direction లో నేరుగా బయటకు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: On the right, the current wire with a compass. The compass reveals the magnetic field B, circling around the wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: right पर current wire और compass है। compass magnetic field B को reveal करता है, जो wire के चारों ओर circle करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: right వైపు current wire తో compass ఉంది. compass magnetic field B ని reveal చేస్తుంది, ఇది wire చుట్టూ circle చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Both are vector fields filling space, and a test object reveals each — the test charge for E, the compass for B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों space को भरने वाले vector fields हैं, और एक test object हर एक को reveal करता है — E के लिए test charge, B के लिए compass।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండూ space ని fill చేసే vector fields, మరియు ఒక test object ప్రతిదాన్ని reveal చేస్తుంది — E కి test charge, B కి compass.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is the whole idea: a still charge fills space with a radial E; a moving charge fills space with a circulating B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही पूरा idea है: एक still charge space को radial E से भरता है; एक moving charge space को circulating B से भरता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే మొత్తం idea: ఒక still charge space ని radial E తో fill చేస్తుంది; ఒక moving charge space ని circulating B తో fill చేస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: The magnetic field is just the electric field — B and E are the same thing
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Left panel: E streams radially OUT from a still charge. Right panel: B circles AROUND a moving charge. Same idea, different source and shape.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: E and B are both vector fields, but E comes from a still charge and points out, while B comes from a moving charge and circles around.

STATE_1.label: The source — switch closes, current flows up

STATE_2.label: Oersted reveal — needle swings to local B

STATE_3.label: No current, no field — needle snaps back

STATE_4.label: The field fills space — compass hops point to point

STATE_5.label: B is a vector field — full 3D rings, all tangent

STATE_6.label: Just like E — radial E (left) vs circulating B (right)

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered this concept when they can say that a magnetic field B is a genuine VECTOR field — a size and a direction at every point of space — whose SOURCE is moving charge (a current), not a magnet; that a charge at rest makes no magnetic field, so when the current stops the field vanishes (the compass snaps back to north); that a compass is a TEST object that REVEALS the field already present at each point, it does not create it, and the field exists everywhere, not only where the compass sits; that near a straight wire B CIRCULATES around the wire — tangent to the circle through each point, perpendicular to the wire, never along it and never radially out; and that this is the magnetic counterpart of the electric field — a still charge fills space with a radial field E revealed by a test charge, while a moving charge fills space with a circulating field B revealed by a compass, the two being alike as vector fields but differing in source (still vs moving charge) and shape (radial vs circulating). (The magnitude B = μ₀I/(2πr) is deliberately NOT assessed here — it is taught by magnetic_field_wire.)

questions.stem: A compass needle deflects when held next to a current-carrying wire. What is the actual SOURCE of the magnetic field that turns the needle?

options.A: The moving charge in the wire — the current
options.B: A small hidden magnet inside or near the wire
options.C: The voltage of the battery, sitting at the ends of the wire
options.D: The metal of the wire itself, because metals are magnetic

questions.tested_idea: The source of a magnetic field is moving charge (current), not a magnet, a voltage, or the wire material.
questions.parallel_form_stem: When current flows through a straight wire and a nearby compass twitches, what is producing the magnetic field?

questions.stem: You switch OFF the current in the wire and the compass needle swings straight back to north. What does this tell you?

options.A: The field is gone — with no moving charge there is no magnetic field
options.B: The field is still there; a magnet remains, the compass just stopped responding
options.C: The wire is now negatively charged, which cancels the field
options.D: The field reversed direction, so the needle happens to read north again

questions.tested_idea: When the current stops the magnetic field vanishes, because a charge at rest produces no magnetic field — confirming the source is moving charge.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Unplugging the charger makes the nearby compass return to north. Why does the magnetic field disappear?

questions.stem: A compass deflects beside a live wire. What did the compass actually DO to the magnetic field?

options.A: It revealed a field that was already present at that point in space
options.B: It created the field — without the compass there would be no field there
options.C: It pulled the field out of the wire toward itself
options.D: It only shows a field exactly where its needle sits and nowhere else

questions.tested_idea: A compass is a test object that reveals the field already present at every point; it does not create the field or limit it to one spot.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Moving the compass to a new point near the wire shows another deflection. What is the compass doing to the field?

questions.stem: Near a long straight current-carrying wire, in which direction does the magnetic field B point at a nearby point?

options.A: It circles AROUND the wire — tangent to the circle through the point, perpendicular to the wire
options.B: Along the wire, in the same direction as the current
options.C: Radially outward from the wire, like spokes from a hub
options.D: Straight toward the wire, pulling everything inward

questions.tested_idea: B circulates around the wire — tangent and perpendicular to the wire, never along it and never radial.
questions.parallel_form_stem: For a straight wire carrying current upward, which way does the magnetic field point at a point beside the wire?

questions.stem: How is the magnetic field B like the electric field E of a point charge?

options.A: Both are vector fields filling space, each revealed by a test object placed in it
options.B: They are identical — B is just another name for the electric field E
options.C: Both point radially outward in straight lines from their source
options.D: Neither has a direction; both are just numbers attached to each point

questions.tested_idea: E and B are alike as vector fields that fill space and are revealed by a test object — the test charge for E, the compass for B.
questions.parallel_form_stem: What do the electric field of a still charge and the magnetic field of a current have in common?

questions.stem: What is the key DIFFERENCE between the sources of the electric field E and the magnetic field B?

options.A: E comes from charge at rest; B comes from charge in motion
options.B: E needs a wire to exist, while B does not need a wire
options.C: E is a vector but B is just a number with no direction
options.D: There is no difference — both come from the same moving charge

questions.tested_idea: The electric field is sourced by static charge while the magnetic field is sourced by moving charge — same kind of object (vector field), different source.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A still charge makes an electric field; a moving charge makes a magnetic field. State the difference in their sources.
